title: Demo: 500-node mesh at hacker conferences
description:
## Goal

Run a 500-node LICHEN mesh at major hacker conferences, demonstrating density handling that competing LoRa meshes can't match.

## Target venues

| Event | Location | Typical attendance | Density challenge |
|-------|----------|-------------------|-------------------|
| DEFCON | Las Vegas | ~30,000 | Multiple ballrooms, RF nightmare |
| HOPE | NYC | ~3,000 | Single venue, more contained |
| CCC | Hamburg | ~17,000 | Congress center, multi-building |

## Hardware

**Primary: LilyGO T-Echo**
- nRF52840 + SX1262 + GPS (L76K) + e-ink display
- GPS enables beacon-free TDMA sync + location features
- E-ink survives battery, shows node status
- Already has LICHEN board support

| Item | Qty | Unit cost | Total |
|------|-----|-----------|-------|
| T-Echo (bulk) | 500 | ~$48 | $24,000 |
| Battery (if not included) | 500 | ~$5 | $2,500 |
| Case (if not included) | 500 | ~$3 | $1,500 |
| Gateway hardware | 8 | ~$100 | $800 |
| Flashing jig | 1 | ~$500 | $500 |
| SDR + display | 1 | ~$300 | $300 |
| Travel/booth | - | - | $3,000 |
| **Total (worst case)** | | | **~$32,600** |
| **Total (if battery+case included)** | | | **~$28,600** |

## Cost recovery

- Sell nodes at $70-80 post-demo
- 500 × $75 = $37,500 gross
- Net: $5,000-9,000 surplus (or reinvest)

## Required features (dependencies)

All scalability epics must land:
- [ ] Density-aware startup (project-LICHEN-inym)
- [ ] Gateway-centric mode (project-LICHEN-dk3c)
- [ ] TDMA (project-LICHEN-i9r0)
- [ ] Multi-channel (project-LICHEN-da2q)
- [ ] Multi-gateway coordination (project-LICHEN-mugl)
- [ ] LR-FHSS optional (project-LICHEN-zd2d)

## Demo scenario

1. **Setup**: Deploy 4 gateways around venue
2. **Distribution**: Hand out 500 pre-flashed T-Echos
3. **Visualization**: Giant screen showing live mesh + GPS positions
4. **Stress test**: Mass boot at talk start, GPS-synced TDMA converges
5. **Roaming**: Walk across venue, show gateway handoff on map
6. **Kill test**: Unplug gateway, watch mesh heal

## Success metrics

- 500 nodes online simultaneously
- <1% packet loss under load
- GPS-synced TDMA: zero collisions
- Live map shows all node positions
- Video goes viral in mesh community

## Timeline

- T-6mo: All features landed and tested in simulation
- T-3mo: Hardware ordered, verify battery/case inclusion
- T-2mo: Flashing pipeline ready
- T-1mo: Integration testing with 50 nodes
- T-1wk: Ship to venue
- T-0: Demo day
